chunks.text: Technical Reports - A. Richard Newton. Graphic Symbol Recognition Toolkit (HHRELCO) Tutorial (M03/50) by H. Hse and A. Richard Newton. Sketched Symbol Recognition Using Zernike Movements (M03/49) by H. Hse and A. Richard Newton. Logic Synthesis for Large Pass Transistor Circuits (M97/26) by P. Buch, A. Narayan, A. Richard Newton and Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli. Engineering Change for Power Optimization Using Global Sensitivity and Synthesis Flexibility (M96/59) by P. Buch, C.K. Lennard and A. Richard Newton. Study of Simulator Sickness and the VOR with Respect to VR (M96/25) by S. Szollar and A. Richard Newton.
chunks.question: What are some technical reports by A. Richard Newton from the early 2000s and 1990s?
chunks.answer: Reports on symbol recognition and logic synthesis.

chunks.text: Logic Synthesis Using Power-Sensitive Don't Care Sets (M96/8) by C.K. Lennard, P. Buch and A. Richard Newton. Microsoft Windows NT and the Competition for Desktop Computing (M94/3) by B. Peters, W.R. Bush and A. Richard Newton. Data-Flow/Event Graphs (M92/24) by G.S. Whitcomb and A. Richard Newton. Implicit Manipulation of Equivalence Classes Using Binary Decision Diagrams (M91/13) by B. Lin and A. Richard Newton. Don't Care Minimization of Multi-Level Sequential Logic Networks (M90/45) by B. Lin and A. Richard Newton.
chunks.question: What topics are covered in Newton's reports from the mid-1990s to early 1990s?
chunks.answer: Logic synthesis, computing competition, and data-flow graphs.

chunks.text: Multiple-Output Shared Transistor Logic (MOSTL) Family Synthesized Using Binary Decision Diagram (M90/21) by T. Sakurai, B. Lin and A. Richard Newton. MOSFET Model Parameter Extraction Based on Fast Simulated Diffusion (M90/20) by T. Sakurai and A. Richard Newton. A Simple MOSFET Model for Circuit Analysis and Its Application to CMOS Gate Delay Analysis and Series-Connected MOSFET Structure (M90/19) by T. Sakurai and A. Richard Newton. Exact Algorithms for Output Encoding, State Assignment and Four-Level Boolean Minimization (M89/8) by S. Devadas and A. Richard Newton. Irredundant Sequential Machines Via Optimal Logic Synthesis (M88/52) by S. Devadas, H.K.T. Ma, A. Richard Newton and Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli.
chunks.question: What are key reports by Newton in the late 1980s to 1990?
chunks.answer: MOSFET models and logic synthesis algorithms.

chunks.text: Easily Testable PLA-Based Finite State Machines (M88/47) by S. Devadas and A. Richard Newton. A Synthesis and Optimization Procedure for Fully Testable Sequential Machines (M88/14) by S. Devadas, Hi Keung Tony Ma, A. Richard Newton and Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli. Test Generation for Sequential Finite State Machines (M87/36) by H-K. T. Ma, S. Devadas, A. Richard Newton and Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli. On the Verification of Sequential Machines at Differing Levels of Abstraction (M86/93) by S. Devadas, H.K. Ma and A. Richard Newton. Algorithms for Hardware Allocation in Data Path Synthesis (M86/92) by S. Devadas and A. Richard Newton. Algorithms for Pipeline Scheduling and Synthesis (M86/91) by S. Devadas and A. Richard Newton. Computer-Aided Design for VLSI Circuits (M86/16) by A. Richard Newton and Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli.
chunks.question: What is the focus of Newton's reports from the mid-1980s?
chunks.answer: Testable machines and CAD for VLSI.
